title: commiphora pseudopaolii
intro: Commiphora pseudopaolii is a species of tree in the Burseraceae family, native to Madagascar, and is also known as the 'African myrrh'. It is known for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its resinous sap. The health benefits of Commiphora pseudopaolii include wound healing, antiseptic properties, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of wounds. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as topical ointments, salves, and infusions, where its resinous sap is often extracted and used to create medicinal products.
